title: Question regarding angels Second Impact
wikitext:
[]: Question regarding angels / Second Impact.             Quote Post by Skullraper » Tue Dec 25, 2007 1:06 am I’m rather puzzled over a statement in the wiki regarding angels and the Second Impact. Maybe someone can help clear it up for me. It says that the 15 angels were scattered around the world when Adam exploded and that they lay dormant for 15 years. However in EoE Misato explains that the Second Impact was orchestrated so as to contain Adam before the other angels awoke. If no angels had existed before the Second Impact then who were these “others”? I had always thought it referred to the 15 and that the Second Impact was done to keep these already born angels from contacting Adam at some point after 2000 when they awoke. That way the damage was minimized. But if no angels existed yet anyway what was the incentive for the Second Impact and preemptively countering the “others”?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Dec 25, 2007 1:13 am Yep, I'm pretty sure that what you mentioned from the wiki was just speculation. Or was it from the CI? If so, it may be the first thing from the CI that directly contradicts something mentioned in the show. Good find.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 25, 2007 1:16 am The origin of the Angels cannot be conclusively answered on the basis of Sandalphon alone. They probably did something to Adam that would cause the other Angels to awaken and then had no choice but to blow her up. EDIT: EoE does have quite a few screwball statements like this one, although some of them are plain mistranslations. (The "Adam came from Lilith" one is a prime goofup)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Dec 25, 2007 1:28 am I'm pretty sure this statement was in the original Japanese, though. So, from what I can tell now, 2I consisted of something along the lines of this: 1. Seele, with knowledge from their SDSS, sent the Katsuragi Expedition to Antarctica with the intention of leading them to find Adam. Seele knew of the other Angels via information from the SDSS and sent the Katsuragi Expedition with the intention of initiating 2I so that the Angels would not awaken, hence their version of Instrumentality would be able to occur more easily. 2. When Adam was awakened, someone from the Expedition voluntarily "gene dived" into Adam and caused 2I. Or something along those lines.

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Tue Dec 25, 2007 1:57 am NAveryW wrote: Yep, I'm pretty sure that what you mentioned from the wiki was just speculation. Or was it from the CI? If so, it may be the first thing from the CI that directly contradicts something mentioned in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Tue Dec 25, 2007 2:12 am NAveryW wrote: I'm pretty sure this statement was in the original Japanese, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Dec 25, 2007 2:31 am ep25' BB translation wrote: Misato: They plan to initiate Third Impact - not with Angels, but by using the Eva series. 15 years ago, Second Impact was caused by humans on purpose. (Flashed shot: Second Impact) But that was in order to minimize the damage by returning Adam (flashed image: Giant of Light) to an embryonic state (flashed image: Embryo in attache case) before the other Angels awoke. (flashed image: graves) Shinji, we humans were born from a being called Lilith, who is a source of life just like Adam. We are the 18th Angel. (flashed image: DNA) The other Angels are possibilities of what we might have become - humans that gave up human form. (flashed image: Kaworu - Rei) Sadly, we had to reject each other - even though we're all human... Listen, Shinji... You must destroy all of the Eva series. It's the only way to stay alive. [Note: The Adam/Lilith line above was verified directly with Director Tsurumaki of Gainax. (Tsurumaki Kazuya => Director of the Eva TV series, Director of movie episode 25' "Air")]

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Tue Dec 25, 2007 2:44 am So it would appear that the statement I read in the wiki is erroneous?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Dec 25, 2007 4:38 am The Angels could've existed but might not have had souls. Adam's awakening would've provided them souls when she opened the Chambers of Guf. It's likely 2I was engineered, to some extent, to lessen the impact this process would have on the Earth as much as possible. I don't think (though I'm not certain) they ever thought they could contain Adam, completely control 2I, and prevent the Angels from being born. But they did seem to think they could control 2I enough so that Adam couldn't completely wipe out the Earth and format it for the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 25, 2007 5:01 am I remember the line, "We will not rely on the power of Adam or the Angels." Perhaps Seele did want the Angels to be Up and Atom so that they could be put to some sort of use. A more likely explanation if Third Impact were to happen, it would probably wake up all the Angels and cause them to flood the Earth and get rid of any of Seele's plans for Godhood accordingly, so they created a space of time to get rid of the Angels. Notice that Seele does not aim at Instrumentality until after getting rid of all the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Dec 25, 2007 9:26 am I believe the current theory is that Adam was in the process of creating the Angels when Lilith landed and the Spear did it's nappy time magic leaving the Angels sitting there without souls like the Evas. 2I was supposed to prevent them from "waking up" (getting souls) but failed horridly at this and ended up causing the conforntaion they were trying to avoid.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 25, 2007 10:35 am Interesting, but Gendo beat the hell out of Antarctica the day before all hell broke loose. He knew that the whole thing would blow up in their faces. Nowhere is it implied that 2I was the result of a backfire. Even the Misato quote does not.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Dec 25, 2007 11:49 am COMMITTEEMAN A: So, the Angels have returned, eh? It's so sudden. COMMITTEEMAN B: It's the same as it was fifteen years ago. A disaster comes as it will, without warning.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 25, 2007 10:16 pm Is that from Episode 02? And from what...the dub or the platinum subs? I don't recall Seele ever showing this sort of surprise about the Angels, but I do know how much they complained about the cost of the Eva and handing it over to Shinji, blah blah blah.

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Tue Dec 25, 2007 10:25 pm Oh yeah, I'm pretty sure they said "The angels have returned" and they didn't like it. Or not. I'm guessing that line was quietly retconned...or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Dec 25, 2007 10:47 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Is that from Episode 02? And from what...the dub or the platinum subs?

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Tue Dec 25, 2007 11:56 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I don't think (though I'm not certain) they ever thought they could contain Adam, completely control 2I, and prevent the Angels from being born. But they did seem to think they could control 2I enough so that Adam couldn't completely wipe out the Earth and format it for the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 26, 2007 8:12 am Skullraper wrote: That’s possible but not definite (correct me if I’m wrong). I interpreted Misato’s line as suggesting that the angels were going to awaken regardless of Adam’s condition and that 2I was done to keep the angels from contacting him.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 26, 2007 9:15 am Reichu wrote: How are the other Angels going to wake up if you leave Mommy asleep and unable to give them any souls? I dunno, lol.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Dec 26, 2007 10:54 am But that blows the idea that the Spear was doing its job by putting Adam to sleep.
